title: The M x N Integration Problem
concept: Before a shared protocol, every AI app needed a custom integration for every tool — M apps times N tools of glue code.

exercises.prompt: When developers want to give their AI apps access to files, databases, and APIs, what is traditionally the biggest bottleneck?

options.text: Writing custom integration code for every tool-to-app pair.
Training larger AI models from scratch.

exercises.explain: This is the first lesson of the unit, introducing the core problem that the Model Context Protocol (MCP) eventually solves: the massive difficulty of connecting models to diverse tools.

cards.title: The Integration Headache
cards.body: Imagine building a smart assistant that needs to read your local files, search GitHub, and query your database. Before standard protocols, you had to write custom API wrappers for every single tool you wanted to connect.

cards.title: The M x N Integration Problem
cards.body: The M x N integration problem describes the scaling bottleneck where every AI application (M) requires custom glue code to connect to every tool or data source (N), leading to exponential work.

cards.title: The Charger Nightmare
cards.body: Imagine if every smartphone manufacturer had to design a unique charger for every single brand of wall outlet in the world. Instead of one standard plug, you would need a bag full of custom adapters just to travel across different rooms.

cards.title: Building Without Standards
cards.body: If you build three AI coding assistants and want them all to read from GitHub, Slack, and Jira, you must write nine separate custom integration pipelines to translate the data for each assistant.

cards.title: AI Can't Auto-Connect Everything
cards.body: You might think AI models can connect to any tool automatically because they are smart. In reality, developers must write rigid API connector code for every tool to fetch data and feed it to the model.

cards.title: Web of Mess vs. Clean Hub
cards.body: See how the architecture changes when you go from chaotic custom links to a standardized connection pattern.

left.text: A messy web of lines connecting every app directly to every database.

right.text: Every app and tool connects to a single standard hub.

exercises.prompt: Why does connecting multiple AI apps to multiple tools create a scaling bottleneck?

options.text: Every app needs a custom connector for every tool, causing integration work to multiply.
AI models refuse to work with more than three tools at a time.
Security firewalls block all connections automatically.
There are too many programmers trying to write the same code.

exercises.explain: The M x N integration problem occurs because every app (M) needs a distinct connector for every tool (N), resulting in M multiplied by N total integrations.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Without a shared protocol, adding one new tool requires writing new integration code for every existing AI application.
exercises.explain: Without a shared protocol, adding a new tool requires updating every single application individually, multiplying the work.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing words to describe the custom integration effort:
exercises.sentence: Before standard protocols, every tool connection required custom glue code.
exercises.explain: Without a shared protocol, developers are forced to write unique 'glue code' for every single pair of applications and tools they want to connect.

exercises.prompt: Identify which description of the M x N problem is written by an AI:

options.text: In the old days, connecting four chat clients to four databases meant writing sixteen separate API wrappers.
Within the landscape of software engineering, the integration of distinct applications with disparate tools historically necessitated customized proprietary bridges.
Think of it like plug shapes: without a USB standard, every gadget needs its own special wall socket.

exercises.explain: Option B is generated by an AI because it uses overly generic, slightly robotic phrasing about 'the landscape of software engineering' to state a basic fact.

exercises.prompt: If a team wants to connect 5 different AI chat applications to 10 different database types without using a shared protocol, how many custom integration codes must be written and maintained?

options.text: The number of integrations decreases as you add more tools.
Every application must write custom code for every single tool, leading to M x N combinations.
AI models automatically figure out how to connect to any database without code.
You only need to write 1 integration regardless of the number of tools.

exercises.explain: Without a unifying standard like MCP, connecting M developer platforms to N developer tools requires writing and maintaining M x N individual adapters, which quickly becomes impossible to scale.
